to Your Own Automations.

The Bus Factor
What if you didn't show up tomorrow?
Does anyone else know where to click when the sales process stops?
The Silent Leak
You're paying hundreds for Zapier.
You don't actually know where the tasks go.
The Documentation Debt
That doc you promised to write.
A year ago. Relay generates it in 30 seconds.

You can't fix what you can't see.

Most Zapier stacks fail for boring reasons.
Not because the automation is "hard" — because nobody documented it, nobody owns it, and the fix happens after the break.

!
Workflows tied to personal emails and credentials nobody remembers
!
No one can explain what a Zap does during an incident
!
Redundant steps burn tasks for no business value
!
If you left tomorrow — could anyone take over?
What Relay gives you
Clarity. A plan. Proof of control.

Run the free Audit to see what's fragile and what's costing you money. Generate the Continuity Report when you need something you can hand off — to a manager, a new owner, or an auditor.

Health score + top priority fixes + plan waste. In seconds.

Plain-English docs + dependency map + troubleshooting + handoff checklist.

Continuity Report

This is not a report.
It's operational insurance.

Something you can send to your manager, hand to a new hire, or file before an audit. Not a Loom. Not a Notion doc you wrote at midnight. A document.

Fix Today Guide
Exact steps for a colleague to resolve an error in under 15 minutes — without calling you.
Maintenance Checklist
What to verify once a month so the system doesn't quietly rot between reviews.
Dependency Map
A map showing how your apps connect — no more guessing what breaks when something changes.

The scenario nobody plans for

What happens when you leave?

Or when the person who built all of this leaves? Most Zapier setups exist only in one person's head. The Continuity Report changes that — plain-English docs your next owner can actually use, without calling you.

Recommended flow
Run the free Audit to see what's broken and what it's costing you. Then generate the Continuity Report when you need to hand it off or document it for someone else.
Start with Audit Generate Report

One export. Two outcomes.

~30 seconds. No upload. Processed locally.

1

Export from Zapier

Settings → Data Management → Export. Download the file.

2

Drop it into Relay

Nothing leaves your browser. Everything runs locally.

3

Download your PDF

Audit or Continuity. A document you can send. In seconds.

Start with the Free Audit
Not for you if

You have 2–3 simple Zaps and it never has to work for anyone else.

  • • You don't care if it breaks occasionally
  • • Nobody else depends on your automations
  • • You've never had to explain a Zap to anyone
This is for you if

You're the one person at the company who owns Zapier — and you feel the weight of that.

  • • You manage 10+ workflows and want to know what's fragile
  • • You'd be embarrassed if it broke during something important
  • • You need something to show your manager — not just a feeling
  • • Someone is leaving, joining, or asking for documentation
Run Free Audit Generate Report

Privacy-first by design

Your Zapier export never leaves your machine.

Relay runs locally using WebAssembly. No uploads. No storage. No way for us to see your data.

Local engine (WASM)

Analysis runs in your browser. Zero server calls.

No upload

Your export stays on your device. We can't access it.

No tracking

No analytics events containing Zap names or credentials.

Client-safe by architecture
Example Output Format

What the reports look like

Generated from a small test export to demonstrate layout and sections.
Your report is produced from your own Zapier data — locally, in your browser.

Risk Audit
Free report

Sample Audit Report

Executive health score, top priority fixes, waste analysis, and plan fit verdict — formatted for internal review or sharing with your manager.

  • ✓ Health Score (0–100)
  • ✓ Top Issues & Priority Actions
  • ✓ Monthly Waste Estimate ($)
  • ✓ Plan Fit Verdict

Sample Continuity Report

The document you send when someone asks "how does all this work?" — plain-English descriptions, dependency map, troubleshooting guide, and sign-off checklist.

Designed for client handoffs, ownership transfers, and audit readiness.

  • ✓ Plain-English per Zap
  • ✓ Dependency Map
  • ✓ Troubleshooting Guide
  • ✓ Handoff Checklist

Example format · Your data stays local

FAQ

Short answers before you try it.

No. Relay processes the file locally in your browser. There is no upload step and no server-side storage.
AUDIT
Use the Audit when you want to know what's fragile, what's wasting money, and what to fix first. Good for internal review, sharing with your manager, or just getting peace of mind.

CONTINUITY REPORT
Use the Continuity Report when someone else needs to understand your automations — a new hire, a client, a manager, or anyone taking over. This is the document you send instead of a Loom.
It's a full documentation bundle — plain-English descriptions, dependency map, troubleshooting guidance, and a handoff checklist. It takes significant processing to produce and it's the kind of document that used to require a consultant or hours of manual work.
Relay uses only what exists in the export and is designed to produce safe-to-share documentation. (Still: review before sending externally, as you would with any export.)
You could. Most people plan to. Almost nobody does. Relay generates it in 30 seconds, from real data, in a format you can immediately hand off.
Yes — that's the whole point. One export, two outputs. Most people run the free Audit first to see the risk picture, then generate the Continuity Report when they need to hand something off or document it for someone else.
Ready?
